- SSE (Sum of Squares Error) is the sum of squared deviations of each observation from its group mean. So SSE = Σ (x_ij - x̄_i)^2 for all i,j.

- MSTR (Mean Square Treatment) is SSTR divided by (k-1), where k is the number of groups (here, k=5).

- MSE (Mean Square Error) is SSE divided by (N - k), where N is total number of observations (20 here).

- F is MSTR / MSE.
